title: NX-OS Control Plane Policing (CoPP) Drops and Class Saturation
exclusions: Pure BGP EVPN neighbor-only telemetry without CoPP counters (use UC-18.4.5); Cisco ACI APIC-contracted dataplane policy (cat-18.1.*); Catalyst 9000 campus control-plane policing (cat-15); third-party NFV telemetry lacking **NX-API** **`/api/mo/sys/copp.json`** (separate ingestion); **Nexus 1000V**/**Nexus 7000** end-of-sale platforms outside this **Nexus 9000** scope; anomaly-only **NDI** exports with no **`cisco:nxos:copp`** metrics (UC-18.4.1 differentiation).
app: **`Splunk Add-on for Cisco Nexus`** (**`Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus`**, [Splunkbase 1917](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1917)); **`cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk`** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); **`Splunk REST API Modular Input`** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for **HTTPS** collectors toward **NX-API**, **NDFC**, **NDI**.
spl:
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=cisco_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:copp" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:health")
earliest=-6h@m
| eval device_name=trim(coalesce(device_name, host, switch_hostname, devicename))
| eval class_name=lower(trim(coalesce(class_name, copp_class, policy_class)))
| eval protocol=upper(trim(coalesce(protocol, proto_family, protocol_name, protocolFamily)))
| eval dropped_pkts_5min=tonumber(coalesce(dropped_pkts_5min, dropped_pkts, violated_pkts_5min, violated_pkts, drop_delta_5m, 0))
| eval cir_pps=tonumber(coalesce(cir_pps, police_cir_pps, copp_cir_pps, cir_rate_pps, 0))
| eval fabric_token=trim(coalesce(fabricName, fabric_name, fabric, ndi_fabric, siteName))
| eval policy_name=trim(coalesce(policy_name, copp_policy_name, attached_policy, "copp-system-policy-strict"))
| eval ndi_site_score=tonumber(coalesce(site_health_score, fabricScore, siteScore, health_score))
| eval syslog_hit=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"COPP-5-COPP_DROPS5|COPP-2-COPP_LIST"),1,0)
| eval ndi_lane=if(sourcetype="cisco:ndi:health" AND (match(lower(_raw),"copp") OR match(lower(_raw),"control.?plane.?polic")),1,0)
| where syslog_hit=1 OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:copp" OR ndi_lane=1 OR isnotnull(dropped_pkts_5min)
| bin span=5m _time
| stats sum(dropped_pkts_5min) as dropped_pkts_5min,
        avg(cir_pps) as cir_pps,
        dc(device_name) as devices_touching_class,
        values(protocol) as protocol,
        latest(policy_name) as policy_name,
        latest(ndi_site_score) as ndi_site_score,
        latest(fabric_token) as fabric_token
    by _time, fabric_token, class_name
| rename _time as window5m
| eval drop_velocity=if(isnotnull(dropped_pkts_5min), dropped_pkts_5min/300, 0)
| eventstats avg(dropped_pkts_5min) as class_baseline_global by class_name
| eval delta_drop=dropped_pkts_5min - class_baseline_global
| eval sigma_proxy=if(class_baseline_global>25, delta_drop/class_baseline_global, 0)
| eval saturating=if(dropped_pkts_5min>(class_baseline_global*1.75) AND dropped_pkts_5min>75,1,0)
| eval proto_bundle=mvjoin(protocol,"|")
| eval attack_lane=case(
    match(proto_bundle,"ARP") AND sigma_proxy>3,"arp_pattern_high_sigma",
    match(proto_bundle,"BGP") AND sigma_proxy>3,"bgp_pattern_high_sigma",
    match(lower(proto_bundle),"lldp|cdp") AND sigma_proxy>2,"discovery_surge_lane",
    saturating=1,"topn_saturating_class",
    1=1,"steady_watch")
| eval policy_drift=case(
    policy_name IN ("copp-system-policy-strict","copp-system-policy-default","copp-system-policy-moderate"), "expected_bundle",
    match(policy_name,"(?i)copp-system-policy"), "vendor_named_custom",
    1=1,"nonstandard_attachment_review")
| eval ndi_stress=if(isnotnull(ndi_site_score) AND ndi_site_score<70 AND saturating=1,1,0)
| sort - dropped_pkts_5min
| head 420
description: Tracks Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS Control Plane Policing (CoPP): Modular QoS CLI (MQC) **`service-policy input`** on **`control-plane`** protects the supervisor CPU via **`copp-system-policy-strict`**, **`copp-system-policy-default`**, **`copp-system-policy-moderate`**, and classes **`copp-system-class-critical`**, **`copp-system-class-important`**, **`copp-system-class-normal`**, **`copp-system-class-redirect`**, **`copp-system-class-monitoring`**, **`copp-system-class-exception`**, **`copp-system-class-undesirable`**, **`class-default`**, plus NX-OS 10.x **`copp-system-class-l3`**. Signals combine syslog **`COPP-5-COPP_DROPS5`**/**`COPP-2-COPP_LIST`**, **`police cir`**/**`police bc`** counters (**`conformed_pkts`**, **`violated_pkts`**, **`dropped_pkts`**), NX-API **`/api/mo/sys/copp.json`**, gNMI **`Cisco-NX-OS-device-yang/copp-state`**, NDFC inventory, NDI **`site_health`**, and Splunk **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**, **`cisco:nxos:copp`**, **`cisco:ndi:health`** on **`facility=local7`**—distinct from BGP EVPN dashboards because work retunes **`class-map`** policing—not overlay neighbors.
value: **`violated_pkts`**/**`dropped_pkts`** in **`copp-system-class-*`** queues starve BGP, OSPF, ARP, or discovery before optics counters twitch. Splunk merges **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** MNEMONICs, **`cisco:nxos:copp`** polls, **`cisco:ndi:health`** posture, and **`cisco:ndfc:fabric`** scope so teams prove **`copp-system-policy-strict`** ceilings, reconnaissance floods, or ISSU overlaps, compressing **`MTTR`** while tracking class **`MTBF`.
implementation: Deploy **Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus** (1917) on forwarders/search heads, schedule **rest_ta** HTTPS inputs toward **/api/mo/sys/copp*.json**, stamp **cisco:nxos:copp**, normalize **FIELDALIAS** chains for **device_name**, **class_name**, and **protocol**, attach **MaintenanceWindow** lookups, then bind **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (7777) workspace tiles. **Quick win:** ship **Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus** syslog and five-minute **cisco:nxos:copp** polls of **/api/mo/sys/copp/inst.json** before wiring NDI.
visualization: (1) **Heatmap** — per-**`class_name`** **`dropped_pkts_5min`** velocity across **`fabric_token`** buckets. (2) **Top-N bar** — saturating **`copp-system-class-*`** classes ranked by **`sigma_proxy`** per fabric. (3) **Line chart** — five-minute **`drop_velocity`** versus rolling **`class_baseline_global`**. (4) **Stacked area** — **`protocol`** facets (ARP vs BGP vs LLDP/CDP) for **`attack_lane`**. (5) **Single value + trend** — **`policy_drift`** counts showing non-default **`copp-system-policy-*`** attachments. (6) **Table** — **`device_name`**, **`class_name`**, **`cir_pps`**, **`dropped_pkts_5min`**, **`ndi_site_score`** for war-room sorting.
schema: CIM **Network_Traffic** (**`bytes`**, **`packets`**, control-plane destined traffic proxies) plus **Performance** (**`cpu`**-adjacent policing) when **`FIELDALIAS`** maps **`dropped_pkts_5min`** into datamodel-friendly fields.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Performance** where **`cpu`** correlates with **`copp-system-class-*`** bursts; accelerate **Network_Traffic** when **gNMI** enriches **`protocol`** reliably for **tstats** rollups.

references.title: Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS Security Configuration Guide — Control Plane Policing
Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS System Management Configuration Guide — Router Policies
RFC 6192 — Protecting the Router Control Plane
Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)
Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights Developer Hub — Fabric Assurance APIs

knownFalsePositives:
**BGP route-refresh bursts** staged after outbound **`route-map`** edits can spike **`copp-system-class-important`** while peers synchronize refresh windows—suppress Sev ladders when **`CHG`** lookups cite scripted **`route-refresh`** maintenance unrelated to hostile **`BGP`** exhaustion hypotheses.

**Supervisor reload choreography** replays **`OSPF`**/**`IS-IS`**/**`BGP`** finite-machine chatter until timers converge—compare **`COPP`** timelines against **`show system reset-reason`** plus boot **`_time`** deltas before blaming steady-state **`violated_pkts`** unrelated to deterministic reload work.

**Leaf turn-up waves** amplify **`LLDP`**/**`CDP`** discovery on fresh fabric links—tie **`discovery_surge_lane`** alerts to **`POAP`** tickets so Splunk distinguishes expansion noise from covert neighbor harvesting narratives around **`T1592`** styled activities.

**Planned strictness relaxations** migrating from **`copp-system-policy-strict`** toward **`copp-system-policy-moderate`** during capacity reviews intentionally enlarge **`police cir`** envelopes—demand CAB proof before **`policy_drift`** macros page as if a surprise attachment event occurred.

**AAA contention** during **`TACACS+`**/**`RADIUS`** brownouts thrashes authentication classes—correlate **`COPP`** bursts with **`AAA-SERVER`**/**`TACACS+`** syslog arcs and **`show aaa server` health before elevating ARP-centric attack stories.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — fabric service entities and episode policies for CoPP-class saturation

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install `**Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus**` (**Splunkbase 1917**) on Heavy Forwarders + Search Heads so `**facility=local7**` retains **`COPP-*` MNEMONIC text for auto-extractions aligned to `props.conf` / `transforms.conf`.
• Schedule `**rest_ta**` (**Splunkbase 1546**) HTTPS collectors (or hardened Python modular inputs) targeting `GET https://<mgmt>/api/mo/sys/copp.json`, `GET .../copp/inst.json`, `GET .../copp/inst/proto-{class}.json` every 120–300 seconds—stamp `**cisco:nxos:copp**`, backoff on HTTP 429/401 per Cisco automation guidance.
• Enable optional **gNMI** dial-out for `**Cisco-NX-OS-device-yang/copp-state**` on **Nexus 93180YC-FX**, **9332C**, **9504**, **9508** roles under test VRFs before production scale.
• Issue **Nexus Dashboard** bearer tokens for `GET https://<nd>/**sedgeapi/v1/site_health**` plus `GET .../**lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics**/{fabric}/inventory` without sharing human passwords—store secrets beside Heavy Forwarder `passwords.conf` bundles.
• Deploy `**cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk**` (**Splunkbase 7777**) so `cisco:nxos:*` tiles share the same sourcetype spellings this UC references.

Step 1 — Data sources
Blend NX-OS planes deliberately: (a) `**cisco:nxos:syslog**` collects `**COPP-5-COPP_DROPS5**` rate-limited drop notices and `**COPP-2-COPP_LIST**` policy binding events emitted when `**service-policy input**` attaches `**copp-system-policy-strict**`/`default`/`moderate` to `**control-plane**`. (b) `cisco:nxos:copp` carries JSON from `**NX-API**` MO trees exposing per-class `**police cir**`, `**police bc**`, conform/exceed/violate counters aligned to CLI `**show policy-map interface control-plane** class {name}` output with `**conformed_pkts**`, `**violated_pkts**`, `**dropped_pkts**`. (c) `**cisco:ndi:health**` surfaces per-site JSON (including embedded CoPP widgets on supported releases) referencing fabric scoring so saturating switches correlate to NDI posture without importing `cisco:ndi:anomaly`. (d) `**cisco:ndfc:fabric**` enriches `**fabric_token**`, `**node_role**` (leaf/spine), `serialNumber`, and policy intent ownership for fleet-wide `copp-system-class-*` attribution. (e) Optional gNMI `Cisco-NX-OS-device-yang/copp-state` streams augment `syslog` gaps where HTTPS collectors lag **ISSU** windows.

Step 2 — Field extractions
Normalize once via `**FIELDALIAS**`/`**EVAL**`: extract `**device_name**` from syslog hostnames or `**mgmt0**` FQDNs; harmonize `**class_name**` tokens (`**copp-system-class-critical**`, `important`, `normal`, `redirect`, `monitoring`, `exception`, `undesirable`, `l3`) plus `**class-default**` aliases; classify `**protocol**` (BGP/OSPF/IS-IS/EIGRP/PIM/IGMP/ARP/CDP/LLDP/STP/HSRP/VRRP); derive `cir_pps`/`bc_pps` numerics from `NX-API` payloads; snapshot `conformed_pkts_5min`, `violated_pkts_5min`, `dropped_pkts_5min` deltas across `**bin span=5m**` windows; persist `policy_name` (`copp-system-policy-strict`, `default`, `moderate`); retain `**vdc_id**` on modular chassis (9504/9508) and `node_role` (leaf, spine) from `cisco:ndfc:fabric`.

Step 3 — Initial validation
Splunk rows must reconcile with CLI truth—(a) compare polled counters against `show policy-map interface control-plane` screenshots archived per incident—(b) run `**show copp diff profile strict profile default**` when debating `strict` versus `default` thresholds—(c) validate NDI CoPP widgets per switch alongside `**sigma_proxy**` anomalies—(d) correlate `**cpumonitor**`/`SYSTEM` syslog families with vendor-neutral SNMP `**cpmCPUTotalTable**` polls—(e) capture `show policy-map interface control-plane class {class}` PDF evidence whenever `attack_lane` fires—(f) verify `NX-API` JSON matches `**show running copp**` class-map references.

Step 4 — SPL building blocks
Author composable macros—(a) `**macro:copp_drop_trend**` timecharts `dropped_pkts_5min` per `device_name`, `class_name`, `window5m`—(b) `**macro:copp_topn_sat**` ranks classes per `fabric_token` where `saturating=1`—(c) `**macro:copp_baseline_delta**` tracks `sigma_proxy` anomalies vs rolling `class_baseline_global`—(d) `**macro:copp_protocol_attack**` highlights ARP vs BGP vs LLDP/CDP surges crossing 3-sigma guardrails—(e) `**macro:copp_policy_drift**` hashes `policy_name` attachments nightly to catch surprise migrations off `copp-system-policy-strict`—(f) `**macro:copp_ndi_bridge**` overlays `ndi_site_score` deterioration whenever `ndi_stress=1`—(g) `**macro:copp_heavy_forwarder_lag**` monitors `_indextime-_time` drift skewing `window5m` aggregates.

Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns
Go deeper before paging—(a) merge `cisco:ndi:health` fabric scores with Splunk lookups so `ndi_stress` rows cite physical `siteName` ownership minus anomaly-category telemetry feeds emphasized elsewhere—(b) nightly `cisco:ndfc:fabric` snapshots feed `policy_baseline` lookups; difference macros highlight drift whenever `inventory` introduces switches lacking `copp-system-policy-*` bundles—(c) join `cmdb_ci` rack elevations via `serialNumber` so `attack_lane` narratives reference aisle positions—(d) schedule Cisco `**openVuln**` pulls tagging CSC identifiers (`**CSCwa***`, `**CSCvy***`) impacting `CoPP` classification—cross-match `Bug-ID` tokens from `show version` syslog—(e) annotate `**T1499.004**` (endpoint denial-of-service) when `violated_pkts` imply CPU starvation plus `**T1592**` when `discovery_surge_lane` aligns with hostile `LLDP`/`CDP` harvesting hypotheses—(f) optional **Splunk ITSI** maps `device_name` entities underneath fabrics—drive episodes whenever `sigma_proxy` breaches `topn_saturating_class` thresholds—(g) predictive `police cir` uplift suggestions derive from ITSI anomaly `severity` layered atop `predict` curves—(h) differentiate `bgp_pattern_high_sigma` storms vs `arp_pattern_high_sigma` flooding using protocol bundle heuristics plus `**BGP-5-**` syslog adjacency corroboration—(i) pair `cisco:ndfc:fabric` ISSU banners with `macro:copp_heavy_forwarder_lag` to excuse collector skew—(j) enrich `evidence` packets with **RFC 6192** references for leadership reviews—(k) maintain lookup tables translating `fabric_token` into PagerDuty bridge numbers during **Sev-1** `attack_lane` fires.

Step 6 — Operational runbook
`CoPP` class drops escalate to **Sev-2** paging when `saturating=1` persists >5 minutes inside the same `window5m` streak for a `device_name`/`class_name` pair—Sev-1 triggers when `arp_pattern_high_sigma` or `bgp_pattern_high_sigma` exceeds 3-sigma proxies simultaneously on ≥2 spine-facing leafs—publish **MTBF** targets per `class_name` (example ≥120 days between sustained violations) and **MTTR** goals (example ≤20 minutes from Splunk acknowledgement to `police cir` mitigation or `class-map` ACL correction)—dashboards must honor `fabric_token` filters (per-fabric tokens) so operations teams avoid cross-site noise—archive CSV proof for PCI/**SOX** reviewers when `T1499.004` narratives appear—quarterly tabletops rehearse `**copp-system-policy-moderate**` fallbacks versus `strict` baselines documented in Cisco Security Configuration Guide chapters—rotate on-call bridges tied to `dataDomain=security-monitoring` stakeholders when `attack_lane` remains `bgp_pattern_high_sigma` despite `BGP` dampening—log post-incident `MTTR` vs class-level MTBF trends for capacity boards reviewing `Nexus 9504`/`9508`** modular headends.

Supplemental depth for production rollouts:
NetOps teams should document staged changes whenever operators lift **police cir** ceilings on **copp-system-class-important** during maintenance because short-lived bursts may still print **violated_pkts** while control-plane authentication recovers. Pair every Splunk drill with archived **show tech-support copp** bundles so Cisco TAC engagements receive the same evidence your search head already indexed. Governance teams can map each **fabric_token** to an on-call bridge stored in ServiceNow fields so incident commanders respect per-fabric isolation when **Sev-1** paging references **attack_lane** spikes rather than fleet-wide optics alarms. Capacity managers should reconcile **Nexus 9504**/**Nexus 9508** supervisor headrooms with policing math because modular systems may present **vdc_id** nuances that confuse single-supervisor assumptions on **Nexus 93180YC-FX** leafs. Finally, rehearse rollback plans for nonstandard **service-policy input** attachments before production weekends so Splunk macros that expect **copp-system-policy-strict** defaults do not misfire when custom class-maps temporarily reference lab-only ACL names.


Runbook rehearsal addenda:
During tabletops, validate that **Splunk** saved searches honor per-**fabric_token** drilldown URLs embedded in Splunkbase 7777 tiles, and confirm that Cisco PSIRT pulls remain throttled so openVuln automation does not starve NX-API collectors on the same Heavy Forwarder. Security operations centers should annotate **T1592** investigations with LLDP/CDP packet captures only when discovery Surge lanes persist after Suppression windows tied to POAP completions, preventing fruitless hunts during approved brownfield expansions. When **cisco:ndi:health** scores recover but **cisco:nxos:copp** counters remain hot, prioritize switch-local policing before trusting controller-only dashboards, because Nexus Dashboard posture can improve while localized class queues still exceed **police bc** buckets.

Fabric assurance continuity checks:
Cross-functional reviewers should verify that SNMP CPU telemetry stays aligned with CoPP saturation narratives by sampling the same five-minute buckets Splunk macros use, preventing mismatched timelines when syslog arrives through alternate relays. Document explicit responsibilities for Nexus Dashboard operators versus rack engineers whenever inventory APIs lag physical deployments, because transient gaps can make policy drift macros appear noisier than operational reality during concurrent POAP waves. Lastly, archive quarterly CSV extracts from the saturation dashboard so auditors comparing MTBF targets against incident tickets can trace each fabric_token escalation path without relying on ephemeral chat transcripts alone.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the quiet governor around each switch brain so routing chatter cannot flood it. When those governors discard traffic too long, we warn your crews early so replies stay quick for everyone relying on that gear.
